senses.definition: relating to something that is before or characteristic of bromide in chemistry; used figuratively to mean something that precedes dullness or inactivity
examples: The scientist described a prebromidic stage of the reaction where no bromide ions were yet involved.
In casual speech, the term prebromidic can be used jokingly to mean before the excitement wears off.

senses.definition: situated or occurring before the bronchi in anatomy; relating to the airways just before the bronchial passages
examples: The prebronchial region was examined to assess early signs of obstruction.
Prebronchial irrigation is sometimes discussed in surgical planning.

senses.definition: existing before or relating to the era or stage when bronze was first used; archaic or historical reference
examples: Artifacts show techniques from a prebronze period.
The museum exhibit covers prebronze metallurgy.

senses.definition: before brute force or theBrute concept; imaginary or rhetorical usage to indicate earliest, crude form
examples: The author sketches a prebrute stage of the conflict.
In the notes, prebrute methods are contrasted with refined techniques.

senses.definition: before the cheek area; relating to the buccal region anterior to the mouth
examples: Prebuccal injections are sometimes discussed in dental contexts.
Anatomists may describe prebuccal spaces in facial anatomy.

senses.definition: an initial or provisional budget proposed before final approval
examples: The council released a prebudget outline to solicit public input.
We discussed some prebudget numbers before the formal budget draft.

senses.definition: relating to or occurring before the formal budget
examples: Prebudget discussions focused on priorities.
Prebudget allocations were provisional.

senses.definition: relating to or affecting finances before a formal budget is set
examples: Prebudgetary estimates guide early planning.
The report includes prebudgetary projections.

senses.definition: multiple items or items prepared before the budget is finalized
examples: The team prepared several prebudgets for comparison.
Prebudgets helped inform the final budget decision.

senses.definition: an early construction or component assembled before final assembly
examples: The parts arrived as a prebuild for the project.
We reviewed a prebuild to ensure compatibility.

senses.definition: to assemble or construct something beforehand
examples: They prebuild modules before shipping.
We prebuild the framework to speed up production.

senses.definition: the stage of construction before the main building phase
examples: Prebuilding work included site clearance and foundation checks.
Construction schedules affected prebuilding activities.

senses.definition: preassembled components or versions used before final build
examples: We evaluated several prebuilds for the software.
Prebuilds helped accelerate the release cycle.

senses.definition: already built or assembled before use
examples: We bought a prebuilt PC.
Prebuilt models save time in prototyping.

senses.definition: behavior or actions that occur before bullying begins; early signs of harassment
examples: Teachers were asked to address prebullying behaviors.
Programs target prebullying to prevent future incidents.

senses.definition: before burlesque; in a preliminary or parodic style preceding burlesque
examples: The play includes a preburlesque scene that sets a comic tone.
A preburlesque introduction mocks serious themes.

senses.definition: the period or process before burning; an early stage of combustion
examples: The engine includes safety safeguards during preburn.
Preburn indicators warned of potential flame-out.

senses.definition: a preliminary counterargument or response prepared before a main argument
examples: She prepared a prebuttal to address potential objections.
In debates, a prebuttal can help steer the discussion.

senses.definition: multiple prebuttals; several preliminary counterarguments
examples: The team prepared several prebuttals for common questions.
Prebuttals helped them deflect critics early.

senses.definition: to buy something before it is released or available; to purchase in advance
examples: We plan to prebuy the latest model when it ships.
Customers can prebuy tickets for the concert.

senses.definition: the act of buying something before it is available
examples: Prebuying has become popular for limited releases.
Prebuying often includes exclusive bonuses.

senses.definition: instances of buying something before it is released
examples: The store had several prebuys for the new gadget.
Prebuys were offered with extra warranties.

senses.definition: the prefix prec- meaning before; used in scientific terms
examples: The word uses the prec- prefix meaning before.
Precambrian is a term with prec- indicating before the Cambrian period.

senses.definition: able to be calculated or predicted beforehand
examples: The risks are precalculable with the right data.
We considered precalculable outcomes in planning.

senses.definition: to calculate something in advance
examples: We precalculate the costs before starting the project.
The model precalculates potential scenarios.

senses.definition: calculated in advance; already computed
examples: The precalculated values saved time during analysis.
Precalculated data can speed up simulations.

senses.definition: calculates something in advance (third-person singular)
examples: The system precalculates outcomes to speed responses.
It precalculates risk scores before presenting results.
